name: UDOO Bolt V8
description: AMD Ryzen Embedded V1605B, high-power x86 SBC

manufacturer.comment: UDOO is a SECO-owned brand; the Bolt V8 is sold via the SECO Shop.

release-date.comment: Kickstarter campaign closed July 2018; broad shipping began late March 2019.

status.comment: Still listed and orderable on the SECO Shop as of 2026-04-26 at $452 USD with 2-5 working day delivery; no successor announced for the V8 SKU itself, but it is a 2018-era design so longevity is unclear.

open-source-hardware.comment: UDOO markets the Bolt as 'open hardware' but the official Resources page only publishes mechanical drawings (top/bottom/3D) and the manual; schematics and PCB files are not released. Per RESEARCH.md guideline (schematics-only does not qualify), marked false.

country-of-origin.comment: SECO S.p.A. is headquartered in Arezzo, Italy.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: UDOO and the SECO Shop quote 3.6 GHz boost; some third-party databases (WikiChip) cite 3.2 GHz boost for the V1605B silicon. Used the vendor's stated maximum for this board.

process-node.comment: GlobalFoundries 14LP; Zen / Raven Ridge embedded variant.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mesa's RadeonSI driver primarily exposes desktop OpenGL on Vega; OpenGL ES capability exists via Mesa but is not a number the vendor advertises for this board. Left null to avoid misreporting a Mesa-version-dependent value.

vulkan-support.comment: Vega 8 is supported by Mesa RADV (open-source Vulkan). AMD has dropped Vega from its proprietary AMDVLK driver, but RADV continues to support it on mainline Mesa.

npu.comment: Zen-era V1000 series has no dedicated NPU.

hw-video-decode.comment: Vega 8 / VCN 1.0 supports H.264, H.265 (HEVC) and VP9 decode. No AV1 decode.

hw-video-encode.comment: VCN 1.0 supports H.264 and H.265 encode.

ram-min-gb.comment: Sold bare-board with no RAM; 4 GB SO-DIMM is the smallest practical kit configuration commonly bundled. Board itself ships without memory.

ram-max-gb.comment: Two SO-DIMM slots, dual-channel DDR4-2400 with ECC support, up to 32 GB total.

ram-type.comment: Standard SO-DIMM DDR4-2400 (not LPDDR4); ECC supported.

microsd-slot.comment: No microSD slot on the Bolt V8.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 Socket 3 Key M 2280 with PCIe x4 Gen 3 for NVMe; an additional M.2 Key B 2260 supports SATA SSDs.

sata.comment: Dedicated SATA III 6 Gbit/s connector on board.

ethernet.comment: Single Gigabit Ethernet via Realtek RTL8111G.

wifi.comment: No Wi-Fi soldered on; an M.2 Key E 2230 slot is provided for an optional Wi-Fi/BT combo module. The Bolt Gear bundle adds one but the bare V8 board does not include it.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; depends on the optional M.2 Key E module the user installs.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: No dedicated USB 2.0 Type-A ports on the rear I/O; USB 2.0 lines are exposed via internal headers.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 2× USB Type-C running USB 3.1 Gen 2 (10 Gbps), with DisplayPort Alt-Mode and USB-PD 3.0.

poe-support.comment: No PoE support; the board uses a 19 V DC barrel jack or USB-C PD.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 2× HDMI 1.4 / 2.0a (CEC). Plus 2× DisplayPort Alt-Mode on the USB-C ports.

max-display-resolution.comment: Vega 8 supports 4K at 60 Hz on HDMI 2.0a; UDOO advertises up to 4 independent 4K displays across the 2× HDMI + 2× USB-C DP-Alt outputs.

displayport.comment: DisplayPort exposed via USB-C DP Alternate Mode (2 ports). No native DP connector.

mipi-dsi.comment: No MIPI-DSI display connector — uncommon on x86 boards.

audio-jack.comment: Realtek ALC888 codec; two 3.5 mm combo jacks (headphone/mic and S/PDIF optical).

gpio-pins.comment: Arduino Leonardo subsystem (ATmega32U4): 23 digital I/O (7 PWM) + 12 analog inputs as accessible Arduino pins. Counted the 23 digital + 10 GPIO from the embedded controller as the user-facing GPIO total; depending on counting method this could also be 23 (Arduino-only) or higher when counting separate EC GPIOs. The header is not RPi-style.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Header layout follows the Arduino Leonardo pinout, not the 40-pin RPi GPIO.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Not RPi HAT compatible mechanically or electrically.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× M.2 M-key (PCIe 3.0 x4) + 1× M.2 B-key (PCIe 2.0 x2) + 1× M.2 E-key (PCIe x1 for Wi-Fi)
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: All PCIe is brought out via M.2 slots; no external PCIe slot or FPC connector.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: Embedded controller exposes 2× UART, 2× I2C, 1× SPI on internal headers, plus the Arduino Leonardo subsystem's own I2C/SPI/UART.

power-input.comment: Either the 19 V/65 W DC barrel jack or USB Type-C Power Delivery; the Bolt Gear kit ships a 65 W barrel adapter.

idle-power-w.comment: Wall-meter idle ~10.5 W (Legit Reviews, Bolt Gear V8). Forum reports cite ~11.2 W; the 10.5 W figure is from a measured Kill-A-Watt reading.

load-power-w.comment: Cinebench R20 multi-threaded peak ~34.5 W at the wall (Legit Reviews). The V1605B SoC alone draws up to ~13.5 W (15 W TDP class).

cooling-required.comment: UDOO ships an official heatsink + fan combo and the Bolt Gear case includes active cooling; sustained Ryzen V1000 load needs a fan.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC battery is included from the factory.

form-factor-class.comment: 12 × 12 cm matches the Nano-ITX standard exactly; UDOO marketing also uses this label.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Mechanical drawings published by UDOO show four mounting holes; not RPi-pattern. Tagged as custom (no Nano-ITX-specific tag in the schema).

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight not published in the official datasheet or overview; only Bolt Gear (cased kit) weight is documented. Left null to avoid guessing.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Standard x86-64 board with AMI UEFI; mainline Linux ≥ 5.2.5 boots out of the box and supports all major peripherals.

official-os-image.comment: UDOO publishes a Bolt-optimized Ubuntu 19.04 image; newer Ubuntu and Windows installs work directly from upstream media.

distros-with-images.comment: Only the Ubuntu image is officially provided by UDOO. Any x86-64 distro installs from generic media via UEFI, but per RESEARCH.md only board-specific images are listed.

long-term-support.comment: UDOO does not publish a stated software support window. The board has been on sale since 2019 and the AMI UEFI receives occasional updates, but no formal LTS commitment.

android-support.comment: No official Android image from UDOO for the Bolt V8.

msrp-base-usd.comment: SECO Shop list price as of 2026-04-26 (excl. VAT/shipping). Original launch price was $459.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Bolt V8 ships in a single SKU (no factory RAM/storage upgrade options); base = top. The Bolt Gear bundle (case, PSU, Wi-Fi module) is a separate product.

available-regions.comment: SECO Shop ships internationally; also distributed via Mouser, Electromaker, and regional resellers (Fab.to.Lab in India, etc.).

typical-lead-time.comment: SECO Shop quotes 2-5 working days delivery as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive online docs, mechanical drawings, Arduino integration docs, BIOS/UEFI guide, and a downloadable hardware manual. Schematics are not published, which prevents a 5.

community-size.comment: UDOO has an active vendor forum but the community is much smaller than RPi/Orange Pi/Rockchip ecosystems.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum with regular activity; no official Discord.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Official accessories cover heatsink+fan, metal case (Bolt Gear), and Wi-Fi module. Limited third-party accessory market — being x86 it leans on standard PC peripherals rather than a HAT/shield ecosystem.
